lines.x: Modern Day
2:48 P.M. \ Clear
Central Sargon, Ibut Region, Nameless Town
No-one here. (We still haven't seen a trace of them. Did they get caught up in something...?)
Strange. This is exactly where we agreed to meet up, right?
Our coordinates check out.
We can't rule out a sandstorm. Let's stay calm.
Sesa... this isn't good.
Hey, now. They're not here yet, but what good is it being so grim? Are we just going to have a staring contest?
Now's not the time for video games, though. Why did you bring that along?
Not important—
Something must be wrong. We should've at least gotten contact at a set time.
Shouldn't we do something or other?
Don't worry so much. What can we even do? The best plan is to just sit tight here, and wait for an answer.
But... it's been too long.
Hold on, there's—
(Sargonian) Good morning.
Heavyrain! Cool it!
Hmph—
S—Sorry, I struck on reflex...
(Sargonian) Oh, your looks deceive. You're quite the quick-tempered girl. This will only damage your first impressions, though...
(Sargonian) This is the Reefsteep Black Market, and we're all in business here. I trust we have words for each other, so no need for an armed conversation, yes?
(Sargonian) What day is today? Why would the 'Sand Soldier' be here?
(Sargonian) Oh, you recognize me?
(Sargonian) The head intel peddler of the Reefsteep market, the puppeteer of the vast majority of all armed conflicts in Ibut, styled by the locals as 'Zuzu.'
(Sargonian) In ancient tongue, it entailed a charm, or perhaps a talisman—
(Sargonian) —but I know what lies behind your new name, 'Sand Soldier.' What's a major player like you up to here?
(Sargonian) Let's not get ahead of ourselves, I just wanted to have a chat with you two. With... er, Rhodes Island.
(Sargonian) Or perhaps... I've disrupted your original itinerary?
(Sargonian) ...Where are our Operators?
Oh... so you're from Sargon too, then. Good. Then let's get right to the point.
Your Operators are safe and sound. Your batch of experimental medical material... also untouched.
Where are they?
Who knows?
You—
Heavyrain, rein it in!
Quite right. Rein it in. You haven't even the capital to start a conflict with me here.
Compared to how valuable your pharmaceutical materials are, a few porters can't be worth much in my concern...
You have your eyes on those materials.
And if I nodded yes?
Let our Operators go. We'll hand over the goods. How about that?
Oh? As simple as that?
But all I took was a few employees... now I'm a little skeptical.
Your goods might've had a small footprint, but they're not cheap. It doesn't seem 'Rhodes Island Pharmaceuticals' is so profligate a company. Would you really be so willing to give them up?
I'll stop you right there. Forget market value. Human life is the most precious thing to us.
A perspective bursting with humanity. But who'll step up to weigh them, then—decide their worth?
Will a certain 'Kal'tsit?'
You nabbed our contract... You know Dr. Kal'tsit?
No, it's a very common name. Over these years, I've sought so many people in error... so many.
Back to our original topic.
Oh, my apologies. Your people are actually waiting right outside, and I have no interest in your goods—
—Heavyrain.
I'll go check now—you stay alert.
As you wish.
Huh! The fixer from the black market... wouldn't come here just to scare our new recruits, would he?
Rhodes Island and the Sargon black market are far from bosom friends. We only had to take this plunge after some of our drugs got jammed up in Columbia.
Oripathy waits for no-one, correct? That's all this is. There should be no conflict between us.
I've heard of you before, the armaments tuner 'Bridge,' having worked for three different arms dealers in the past. A fine reputation. Oh, I believe you're called 'Sesa' now?
Really, you make too much of me.
Well, then, what gives you an interest in Rhodes Island?
During a routine probing of accounts, a completely watertight transaction came to light. You must know, not one sheaf of gold in the Reefsteep passes out of my notice.
And?
No, no cause for worry. You haven't exposed any leverage, and as I've already said, your transaction doesn't interest me. It's just the... signature, on this contract, that I mind.
Come. I have time to spare today. Tell me, what kind of place is Rhodes Island Pharmaceuticals?

lines.st: 'Trek upon Terra, as millions of lives.'

lines.x: 22 Years Ago
1:09 P.M. \ Clear
Central Sargon, Ibut Region, the Town of Redhorn
They're firing! Ambush! Ambush!
Withdraw our cov—aghh—
Are they mad?! With this many civilians around?!
Quick! Hide here!
Shit, close. Thanks.
—Fire, fire! My house—ukh— (The sound of a throat being pierced.)
Hurry! Hurry!!
Eyaaargh—
Too many civilians getting caught up...! Are they trying to pull the Lord Ameer of Ibut's whole army in!?
The Lord Ameer's army? How do you know they weren't the army in disguise?
You mean—
Look at this.
The red mark...? You picked this off one of the corpses—?
I did. We've been fooled by our own. The situation here is a complete mess now. You might not even get it if I gave you the whole lecture—
It's way more than just us who wanted to ransack that squad. Dammit. Grab it and turn it into cash, my ass. This is a full-blown war now—
The sentry sent me all the coordinates before going black. There's four different identifiers all crawling this one area!
Do you understand what this means? Either we break through the line and earn big, or we still have the chance to surrender right now!
—Goddamnit! This is insane!
*pant*... *pant*... *cough*, *cough* *cough*...
...should be... alone here...
*cough*...
...wh—who?
A child? Why are you carrying a body... eugh, get out of here already!
Hold on... your clothes! You're a Columbian!? You're from the 'Sand Soldiers'!
B8 here, we have a survivor, a Liberi child, carrying a grown man's body—
Hey, hold it right there!
Alright, kids, you all heard that. The 'Sand Soldiers' still have one left alive! We need to bring him over!

There's still a chance our target fell into someone else's hands. Just in case, kill every last one you come across. Doesn't matter if they're the Lord Ameer's. Even if Padishah Murad himself is here, he has to die too.
Remember, a silver box. I want to be sitting on that singing before the sun goes down.
We've intercepted their comms band. It seems the 'Sand Soldiers' still have a survivor.
A child, carrying a body, probably a comrade. Think he's got what Padishah's after?
Not one team's retreated from that town yet. Chances are they're all still looking for their target.
Of course. Everyone swarming that tiny of a town at once is just going to add to the chaos, waste time and nothing else.
Hold on, our Caster's gotten news. Something's up...
What?
There's a freight truck... registration Tkaronto, Columbia... parked near the Central Smoke bazaar.
A woman and a monster? Monster? What monster—oh.
Line cut off. He gets careless too?

No, his ambush point is at least a kilometer out from the bazaar. It shouldn't have been that fast. Not unless whatever team this is knew the topography here like the backs of their hands... or maybe, they're just experienced enough to blow anyone off the sand.
Could it be someone from Vouivre? Or some other cutthroat here on Columbia's dime?
Let's watch this unfold first.
*pant*... *pant*...
*cough* *cough*... Why did... Teacher... *cough* *cough*!
Our target's around here! He can't get that far carrying a body—
Tch! We've got trouble! No hesitating! Kill!
...am... am I safe...?
...Teacher, Teacher... come on, wake up...
The... I can't stop the blood... you still haven't taught me so much... what do you want me to do...
...Target found.
Don't move! One more inch and I'll cut your head off!
Scared stupid, aren't you, talking to a dead guy? Uh-huh?
Put that idiot on your back down and tell me, where is it!?
I don't know...
Mmph—
Don't try and fool me, you worthless... huh?
Is this... the 'Sand Soldier' leader's body? What are you... wait, do you have it on you?
—Don't touch him!
Gah! Who do you think you are—piss off!!
Ugh...
The silver combination box! So here it is... heheh, hahahah!
Let go of it! Get—Get out of my way!
Heh, now we've got the target, we don't need to leave any survivors.
Don't blame me, kid. This is my job—
Wha—What the hell is—?!
Grrr...
What is that—hey, is anyone there?! I've run into an unidenti—
(Shrieks)
—Ugh!
Machine... no, creature?! What the hell kind of thing are you?!
Don't get any—why aren't my bolts doing anything—don't get any closer! Any closer and I'll kill you!
(Snickers)
Before his eyes, the monster skewers the ruffian.
It lightly unfolds its body, like a stretch in the early morning.
...Mon3tr.
(Responds)
Ah... ah...
The boy is frozen in place, having long lost the voice to cry out.
He holds only an ice-cold carcass, the blood of the dead clotting on his chest, reminiscent of a flower.
Elliot, you're safe.
Who... who are you... How do you know me?
I know every last esteemed scientist of the Blaine Formative Technologies Research Institute.
But none of you know what kind of plot this whole affair has been drawn into. I'm here to stop you, and here to protect you.
Protect...?
You're already late... too late...
You didn't let your fine teacher's legacy fall into those conspirators' hands. You've done well enough.
What do you mean, protect...
—You want to protect these! The blueprint, the sample, not me! And not Teacher!
You're just like them! Teacher's dead! All the people who were helping us are dead too! You're all just fighting over this case—you—you—*cough* *cough*, *cough*—
Adviser, we've confirmed three mercenary teams hired by lords ameer across the map, as well as one Sarkaz unit on a cliff outside the town, affiliation unknown for now.
According to our plans, we still have seven minutes.
I've found one survivor, sample in hand.
The evac route is secure.
Converge in three minutes.
Tell me, Elliot. The Vouivre soldier you're carrying on your back: what did he die for?
...Shut up...
Right now, you stand here, you live, and you carry what every mercenary here wants.
The squad operated under the name 'Sand Soldiers'... but in actuality, all that came to Sargon was a research team, and a typical security force from Tkaronto.
Whether or not he persisted in his duty until the end, whether or not he paid mind to your safety until the end, didn't he entrust a deep responsibility to you?
Whether or not because of your own immature temperament, are you going to make a total waste of all your soldiers' full efforts?
I only need an answer.
As long as I can ensure that this prototype blueprint doesn't fall into any lord ameer's hands, I will not go out of my way to mind anything else. I hope for you to understand this.
High alert. You still aren't willing to speak much.
But I remember... the man on your back is Professor Thorne. Blaine Formative Technologies Research Institute, Head Researcher.
He was not a true soldier. Yet a true soldier is what he is.
You...
Just put him down. Or you could be buried together with him in the yellow dust.
I trust he believed his death had a purpose, and you should not squander his sacrifice.
No...
He's already dead.
No! This isn't your business!
At least give him a decent burial.
...Mon3tr.
(Hissing)
What's... What's that ball of light?
Lower your head.
Ngh—!
...You... You want me to bury Teacher in here?
Professor Thorne is an old friend of mine. The situation is abnormal. We have no chance to more solemnly address his sacrifice.
Every person's life is an endless war.
On certain motives, his ancestors left Sargon. Wandering again and again, he found a life he could call his own, yet in the end he returned to Sargon's barrens.
His cause, his just heart, his thirst for knowledge all arrived at their terminus.
The Professor Thorne I knew was in no way unversed in life's affairs. Candidly, he confronted his own death, and until the very end resisted.
Elliot, just put him down.
This is respect for the deceased. Surely you understand, he wouldn't have wished to become a burden on your path?
Okay... okay...
(Ancient Vouivran language) May his soul, with the long river, return.
Vouivran...? Are—Are you from Vouivre?
(Ancient Vouivran language) May he, as the gravel, pass into eternal tranquility.
(Ancient Vouivran language) May he ever hear his homeland's whispers. May he, at the other shore, meet the great waves.
(Ancient Vouivran language) Our friend rests eternal here. He shall return to the boundless cycle of rebirth.
As the woman bowed her head and prayed, it was as if the fire of war, too, paused for this moment.
...Time is running short. We've paid what respect we can. Now, you must make a choice.
(A low, alert sounding.)
Correct, they are many, but their formation is in loose disarray. As an armed force, they're not in one heart and mind.
Before they find us, they'll make their own grave.
...I remember, now... I remember I saw your face before, somewhere... long ago... Teacher introduced you to me...
I thought you were only a theoretical researcher...
I remember... Your name is...
Kal'tsit...?
Mm.
You have quite a good memory, Elliot.

lines.st: The young researcher, Elliot Glover, pays no attention to the mysterious woman as she leaves through the door.
In a daze, he gazes at the soil that half-buried his beloved teacher, the air scattered with dust.
He thinks to himself: I haven't even the strength to cremate my teacher's remains. It leads him, for an instant, to mourn.
Until the sound of warfare resumes once more.

lines.x: 22 Years Ago
3:09 P.M. \ Clear
Central Sargon, Ibut Region
Adviser.
Confirm retrieval of target, and before any other teams form up and cut us off, pull out of here.
...Understood.
This is the op team. Advanced-Ops Adviser has retrieved the 'Sand Soldier' survivor along with target material.
Acknowledged.
Adviser, recon team has ensured the safety of our route.
Alright, then. Let's go.
Uh, Mr. Elliot, please kindly give that case to us.
The boy tightly clutches the case in his hands.
Adviser?
Leave him be.
Pardon me, but we should confirm whether our objective materials are intact. And ensure 'no matter the circumstances,' they're safe under our grasp.
You think he'll run?
No, of course... alright... understood.
Yes.
Uh, why did we stop?
Seems we broke down.
What's wrong? Caster, come take a look.
Couldn't be out of fuel, right... did nobody inspect before we left base? Who was in charge?
J5 from the Stouthammer Squad was on vehicle inspection, but they're all on the recon team. What should we do? Get in touch with them?
Elliot.
Huh?
Keep that case safe, and lower your head.
...What?
What just exploded—woah!
Recon team?! Hey, can you hear us? Tell your Casters to stop! It's us, wait, it's us!
Mon3tr.
Th—That thing in your hand—
(With vigor, it rouses itself.)
No, don't be rash.
Protect this child.
(Responds)
Hhhh—
What—What's happening?! Weren't they with you?! Here to help us?!
Stay alert, Mon3tr.
Agh—
(Dissatisfied)
Dammit! They split as cover to set up the whole goddamn circus...!!
Attention, Riverblade Squad, all members of Riverblade Squad!
The recon team has overtly attacked us, scratching every—clause of the contract! Caster division, ready up! Snipers, confirm bearings, and prepare to attack!
You all copy! The Stouthammer Squad has turned traitor! Fight back, open fire!!
In the barrens, escaping an armed motorcade on foot isn't much of a sagacious choice.
Stay here. This is the safest option.
Ugh...
Did you plan this, too?
No. War can bring great profit to Columbia, but nobody wants that profit in anyone else's pocket. Dividing up interests isn't just about size, and satisfying everyone is extremely difficult.
As their advanced operations adviser, I've given sufficient counsel.
Including all 'betrayal' possibility.
Right now, there's a war going on outside...
And you're just going to sit in here, unconcerned?
I have to remind you, Elliot, you also sit here, receiving my protection at that.
No... it's this box. They don't have the guts to attack us. I'm the one who's your protection, 'Adviser.'
(Discontentedly, it gazes afar.)
I don't intend to retort. They absolutely won't dare destroy an Arts prototype that could convert half of Sargon's rainforests into barrens.
Despite its disguise as... an engine on an all-new power source.
What—What are you going on about...
Adviser! We can't hold out for much longer! We need to pull back immediately, and change course!
Please, bring the survivor with us! We'll ensure your safety!
Understood.
How many withdrew?
Less than twenty. Wretched losses.
Let me say again, Captain, scores of our brothers just died.
Thanks to them pulling a heel turn out of their assholes in the middle of an operation! The company's gonna send people to wipe every last one of 'em off the goddamn map!!
I... don't know about that. If the Stouthammers had this planned in advance, I'm worried the company hasn't got the reach into Sargon...
Maybe they made a deal with some lord ameer. Or maybe, they just never...
Ugh... the adviser and the kid?
Right over there.
Let me... let me talk with them alone.
Adviser.
Before we entered Sargon, I gave you the heads-up.
The military needed to light a fire in Sargon, and then wait for war to bring riches their way.
Factories, mines, the arms trade—every second of its existence, Columbia has been gnawing away at this ancient country, strengthening itself.
So its military pulled a move, brought about the birth of the 'Sand Soldiers,' had them bring scientific researchers into Sargon... to carry out some 'tech support.'
—But the Bureau doesn't take things that way at all.
To be precise, it's the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Arts Units and Originium and its background desires that don't take things that way.

Perhaps Sargon is a backwards, rotting country to some people's eyes, but its ancient wealth and power still suffices to command servitude of all the dust in its sky, to become the desert under the Empire's gaze.
I have to say, the Bureau's viewpoint is correct.
One portion of Columbia's people has grown narrow-minded with its ballooning riches and power. They mistakenly think they can cause wanton strife in Sargon without paying a price.
Any one Padishah's maneuvering is enough to influence those Columbian entrepreneurs' stock prices, yet they turn a blind eye to Sargon's vast might.
The policymakers are free to be ignorant, and failure in judgment won't harm their core interests, but you yourselves need to know just what it is you're doing.
At least, you should know, this and your life or death are symbiotic.
The Padishahs are the Sargon Empire's chief governors at the core, there to control their lords ameer. Would they... have anything to do with the company?
You know what it is the company wants, correct?

Certain high-ranking members of the military have seized some of Blaine Formative Technologies' principal shareholders, exporting these things to Sargon under unofficial cover in order to fulfill a deal with a lord ameer.
And what they truly desire is to employ these simple, crude methods to set off a power struggle within Ibut. Because that will turn profit.
But the Bureau believes that if the war spreads to the heart of the empire, every 'moneymaking' design will become fly ash.
So, the Bureau wants us to retrieve the target, so as to stop the military brass from going a step too far. That I get, but—

But the military's not stupid. Its spies within the company—perhaps you've already guessed who—have bribed the Stouthammer Squad to ensure the plans will remain in Sargon, and then a certain lord ameer can set off war.
They had the balls?!
Aside from the operation advisers that aren't me, all silently acknowledge this string of activity inside the company.
The Chief of Business has no plans to stand on any side, allowing both parties to hold each other back. It might even bring in bonus grey income, after all.
None of this is any kid's business.
I—
Elliot Glover. Only 13 when he skipped a few grades and graduated, soon entering an academy for sciences and taking up studies in Originium technology.
Three years after, this young genius was picked out by Professor Thorne, to become in all respects his personal assistant.
Elliot is a member of the 'Sand Soldiers.' A research team crowned as soldiers who had no idea at all what awaited them in Sargon.
They died tragically in the sandstorm without a single clue about anything, and none of us can deprive their last survivor of learning the complete picture.
Fine, I get it. No skin off my nose.
Now where were we? Oh, yeah, Bureau and the military pressured the company, and then in Sargon, all the lords ameer clashed...
Heheh. We were betrayed from the very beginning. And we jumped into this war knowing scratch all...
Where'd they get the balls to do this shit... Now they've thrown us into the desert like garbage, who can still guarantee things'll go how they want...?
The company never cared about what ultimately ended up in whose hands. Regardless of which of you survived to complete the mission, they'd still pocket a reward.
To be precise, even if you all died right here, it wouldn't affect a single coin that falls into their pockets.
Because the ever-eyeing lords ameer and the Padishahs would've helped you settle and follow up on things otherwise, it's only natural they brought in the chaos.
The moment you were first drawn into Sargon by that enormous reward, you were already cornered.
Sorry, Adviser.

You warned us out of the kindness of your heart, and you gave our op everything you had too. I respect you a hell of a lot more than I do those cigar munchers, all making up absolute shit at their meeting tables.
But maybe... we still have one other option. Right now, pull out your arms, Adviser. I hope this decisive battle can preserve some dignity.
You're going to kill her...!
Why not? That's what our original job really was, after all.

Don't lose your head, mercenary. I know you've received secret orders from the Chief of Business, but I also trust you understand that right now, following things to the letter will only cut off your own way out.
Yeah, you've always given me an omniscient kind of impression...
The company sold us out, the Chief of Business sacked us like pawns, the lords ameer, the Padishahs and the Columbian military are all itching to kill us and snatch the goods—
—but we're mercs, and that's just morning coffee to us. As long as I can still make bank, I have no shits to give.
What a pity.
I'm just the smallest fry in this whole whirlwind of crisis, but whether it's in the Bureau's interest or the military's—whatever. All that shit makes me dizzy—
Even the company thinks you shouldn't hang around here any longer, Adviser.
This is a good chance. A good chance to make a senior adviser in Sargon disappear. Some folks paid me a lot of money—heh, and I couldn't say if they were the same who bribed the Stouthammers.
But none of that matters. I just wanna ask you one last thing... You... You've done so much. What was it all for, in the end?

Columbia, Sargon, and perhaps Victoria as well. Uncountable powers all wrapped up in this. And though their lack of foresight led us here, all their innumerable machinations point towards one endgame.
—War.
And war will bring ruin.
I simply try to stop this all from coming, despite how weak my influence is.
I... I don't know whether I oughta believe you... Maybe as long as I complete the Chief's orders and kill you, the company'll let us back in...

Who would trust sixteen mercenaries forced to hole up in a cave, a scientist's boy assistant and a mercenary company's operations adviser? Who would willingly sit down with them to discuss where millions of lives are headed?
I'll give you one final heads-up. Even if you make the Chief of Business happy, and make an obstructive adviser vanish in the middle of Sargon, it will not mean you can return home safe.

Every hired hand in these yellow dusts believes you've made off with the final legacy of the 'Sand Soldiers'... and they will make the choice to exterminate a flock of Columbians who offended their lord ameer's graces.
I—
After you kill me, you want to use Elliot as a bargaining chip, but would you have the leeway for a peaceful dialogue?
—No, you're right. I've got no chance of making any kind of 'deal' with the people who just killed my brothers. I don't work with the big picture like that.
Alright. You've convinced me, Adviser. I'll put down my crossbow, and you get that... uh, your pet out of sight a little.
(A low roar, as if to menace.)
How are you going to proceed?
If we keep working together, will you ensure we return to Columbia?
Precisely the opposite. If you truly keep working with me, you will only add to the dangers you face.
But at the least, I can plot a route for you, and help you stow away into Minos. This is the safest path.
'The safest path.' And you're not even gonna go with us?
My goal lies elsewhere.
There's no way you'd say you prepped this route just for us, right?
Don't misunderstand. This is just for those who can get out of here alive, regardless of who they are.
Sounds a little like salvation, huh?
There's a hint of arrogance behind that word. If you have any misgivings, just treat this as a deal.
Alright, alright. Last time I didn't trust you we ended up right in the frying pan. Maybe this time I oughta be a little smarter...
This is a deal, Adviser. I want to use both your lives, to buy the lives of me and the brothers I have left.
Sixteen for two. Very cost-effective.
Damn right. Cost-effective. Hah. You saw this day coming from miles away, didn't you?
Where are we... heading?
Where are we... I... I want to...
There's a local place for dealings, which the local arms dealers and underground Originium goods merchants all call the 'Reefsteep Black Market'.
When we find that market, its stowaways can help us out.
What's on your mind?
I'm thinking about... I...
I just... just left Teacher's body... in that makeshift...
I should've... I... the blood, and the screaming... nngh, ulp...
*cough*, *cough* *cough* *cough*—
Adviser's hit the road.
What now?
Here's the map for the route the Adviser gave us. Not just the names of all the towns, but any Messenger stations we can use too. Can you guess how much this map'd be worth alone?
Just where in holy hell did she come from?
Hell knows! But right now, she's the single straw we've got to grab at.

Tear up all identifying marks. Can't keep using these Columbian cars anymore. We'll head to the closest city on foot. Should be about thirty kilometers, then we'll rent a new ride and get out of here.
And we escape just like that?
I know what you're thinking... but we still aren't ready for payback. We'll hide out for a bit. Then we can go back, deal with all those traitors companyside—
—Who's there?!
Heh.
So you let the 'Sand Soldier' go... Now there's a twist.
—Enemy raid! Snipers, rea—
Leave three alive. Get the casters ready to grill them.
Deal with all the rest.
Have you calmed down some?
Counting researchers, the 'Sand Soldiers' were on the scale of a hundred people when they left Tkaronto.
You just experienced a massacre. This may be difficult, but you need to return to form as soon as possible.
Just what... do you intend to do?
Retrieve the Arts prototype plans in your embrace. Assuming no mishaps, there should also still be an Originium crystal sample in there.
Ngh...!
This will only delay the outbreak of war, but it will give us enough time. Enough to afford a few of my colleagues in Columbia the chance to try and change course.
You hardly even know what that case could unleash.
You can't! What kind of sick joke?! This is everything the Institute worked for!
Whether you trust me or not, I know full well just how important, how weighty, results paid for with countless lives are to a theoretical scientist.
But I also know, if this thing termed an 'Advanced Originium-Powered Arts Conversion Device' is given different application, the sheer harm it can bring about.
Ugh...
Many were aware of this. Naturally, this included your teacher.
Y-You don't mean to say Teacher knew we were doing wrong?!
How dare you suggest—
So this is Sargon! Ah, a time-old charm of a country... look around here, at the heart of the wild-grown desert, no dependence on nomadic cities, still so full of life!
It is, Teacher.
Hm? Elliot, your mind's wandering. Come here, let me brush that gravel off your head.
Ah... th-thank you, Teacher.
Teacher, if you were this caring of the women who tried to woo you, I don't think your family would urge you so hard to get married.
Haha. Don't poke your teacher where it hurts, or else you'll be assigned to double next month's experiments.
Ugh...
Teacher?

Ah, not much, just something that popped into my head. The mountain range at the end of the barrenlands, and the rainforest on the other side, must both be bursting with life too in their own ways, totally unlike the barrens we're in.
In that rainforest... could there reside people just as unlike those here? What kind of life do they lead, then? How does it fare?
The rainforest? Why all the sudden thought, Teacher?
Elliot. We believe our undertakings are for the benefit of ever more of humanity, for those still without the blessings of Originium technology.
But at times, the fruits of our research may not be implemented as we intended. Perhaps from the start, we choose a path wrong, excessively wrong, and when we realize it, we are powerless to redeem.
Huh?
Haha. Just supposing, provided a day like that dawned—
—We'd have to try and stop this all in its tracks.
Mr... Thorne, he...
I... No, it can't be, he was deceived... he...
Thorne's research had been rigged from the start.
The capital bounced around to land in his hands, disguised as an exciting investment in the new energy market, perfectly unscrupulous, giving nobody any doubts.
I'd warned him before. But perhaps the experiments' success and advancement made him put his own misgivings aside.
No one is perfect, Elliot, but your labors will not become a catalyst of conflict and death. I came here to ensure that, and I need your assistance.
Right. You have five minutes to gather your thoughts, and then we'll leave.
Huh?
You want me to go with you?

You'd only die in the barrens otherwise. I won't deny your gift for the mathematical and applied Originium fields, but you don't have even the first idea what vegetation can offer you any moisture content.
I don't want the last young one who knows the truth to senselessly pass away in front of me.
Of course, if you feel your calling is at its end, that you have no need to press onward, I can arrange for your refuge in the Sargon countryside.
Just... Just who in the world are you? Why do you speak of everything so... downplayed?
I'm presently an Advanced Operations Adviser and Arts Instructor for the Westin Security Company, Columbia... If I have to repeat myself once more to you.
I am Kal'tsit.

lines.st: Kal'tsit.

lines.x: Kal'tsit. Kal'tsit.
Hm... look at this contract...
What a beautiful signature it is.

lines.st: She mediated between Columbia and Sargon. She toyed with the lords ameer and mercenaries in the palm of her hand.
She said she came to prevent war, to prevent Sargon's self-destruction—heh, what arresting ambitions.
They weren't her truest thoughts at all. Her preparations, profound, far-reaching, not to be known by anyone else, influenced the fates of many.
And we can scarcely see how long the path she walked was, or where it led.

eps.name: A Bolt From the Blue Oasis

lines.x: 17 Years Ago
3:09 P.M. \ Clear
Ursus, 147km northwest from the main course of nomadic city Chernobog, village
A hamlet...
Quick, quick, the doctor says to get warm water!
Where would we find warm water so easily? Ask Marfa, ask if her family still has any water they boiled this morning.
Emperor above, we beg you, bless our old dedushka...
Excuse me... What's happening here; who's the doctor you mentioned?
Oh, you're... who are you?

Where did you come from... your clothes mean you're from the city, right? Sorry, sorry, we're busy aiding someone right now... If you don't mind, come with me this way, at least you'll have a place to sit.
It's okay... I came here to find that doctor, as it happens. What's her name?
You... you're not here to...
She's just a shady doctor. Do you have great trust in her?
But she... she's all we have who can save our dedushka. I beg you, I'm begging you, at least just for now—she's only a doctor here to save us.
I'm not here to seize her. I just have a little business with her.
Could you show me the way?
I... I don't know if...
I'm a doctor too. Perhaps I can be of help.
Oh, even a burdenbeast would know you're a scholar, seeing your clothes...
You can tell Dr. Kal'tsit something first. Tell her, her student Lillia's here to visit her, and ask her if she needs any assistance or not.
Ah, so you know the doctor's name, and you're Lillia, is that right? Good, good, truly a wonderful name. Wait there for a moment, I'll go right now.
Ay, hey! Don't forget to ask Marfa, bring warm water over!
Ugh, ughah...
Grit your teeth, and keep holding on.
Ugheaaagh—grh—
...Kal'tsit...
What's going on here?
Attacked by a wild beast. This is just emergency treatment.
Knife.
Ah, er... where—where was it... I can't find...
Here. Hand.
An Infected creature?
Correct.
Any risk of Infection?
Most Infected creatures are cleaner than people imagine. By the toothmarks, a tuskbeast roaming the vicinity.
But if it's come into contact with active Originium...
Infected creatures may have methods to coexist with pathogens, but humans do not.
The situation is severe. This is not Originium infection alone. Additional necrosis symptoms are present.
Hurts... so much...
Calm down, now. Deep breaths.
Hphhh—haaah—hphhh—
Difficulty breathing, likely thrombosis. All symptomatic of acute Oripathy.
But—But doesn't Oripathy make crystals grow out of your skin?! Our dedushka's fine, see, isn't he?!

In certain circumstances, the source of infection ends up rapidly circulating through the blood, and won't present visible Originium crystals like regular cases of Oripathy do. Infection symptoms intensify within a short time, very quickly.
If we don't suppress it via drugs... mm.
Emperor above, Emperor above...
You've learned a lot.
Syringes are... inside the gray medical kit.
Here... You carried a suppressant like this with you?
There's always times of need.
Wait... *cough* *cough* Do—Doctor... is this—expensive?
Thinking about that at a time like this?!
It's affordable. You have no need to worry. Our top priority right now is suppressing your acute Oripathic responses, and prevent an Originium crystal dust outburst.
You don't want the others in the village to suffer because of you, correct?
Ugh!
Steady breaths. Relax.
You'll be fine.
Ngh, alright...
Hfff—haaah—
Urgh... hff... do—doctor... I don't want to... become Inf... *cough* *cough*... Infected...
I'm very sorry.
I... *sob*... I... *cough* *cough*... *cough* *hack* *cough*...
Suffocation. We don't have a respirator...
We have nothing here, but that doesn't mean we can do nothing. Bring the inhaler over.
Doctor! Doctor! I'm begging you, save this dedushka of ours, damn it!
The moment Originium dust permeated his wounds, it was inevitable he'd become an Infected, and all I can do is strive to extend his life.
Hck, *cough*, *hack* *cough* *cough*—huff, hahh—
Has the suppressant still not taken effect?!
Considering the patient's age, the suppressant itself was a tremendous risk. Hold your nerve.
...hah... hah...
Breathing has stabilized some...
Is he better now? Is his sickness gone?
In terms of his acute symptoms, possibly. But if you mean his Oripathy, we can do nothing. No one can do anything.
How could this... Then if the man from the Patrol Unit comes by...
Then just—Then just give me... give me up! I only had so long left anyway... Infected? Fine, so be it... Just as long as you don't let the eggheads in here to run their mouths...
No! We wouldn't, we wouldn't, we just have to hide you. We won't let anyone find you. We'll all hide you safely.
Doctor, thank you so much, doctor, things might not have turned out best, but we... well, we don't have much to repay you with either...
This is merely a doctor's duty. The path ahead of becoming an Infected... is what you truly need to consider. I'm very sorry, that circumstances couldn't permit me to do more.
Suppressant is effective, but wounds are still infected. It may lead to worsening of thrombosis... We may have to operate and excise...
Your judgment is correct. Good sir. Please bear with this.
Agh—alright... alright... eaaargh, ungh—!
Sorry about that, Lillia.
Long time no see, Director Kal'tsit.
Long time no see. Let's walk and talk.
Perhaps... you're not enthused about conversing with me like this after that bloodbath, but from the bottom of my heart, I'm glad you're still alive.
So after that, you hid here the whole time?
From the hill over there, you can see out to Chernobog's skyscrapers. If it were me, I wouldn't opt to shelter so near a cityway.
I'm only stopping over for the moment, nothing more.
What... What have you been doing, ever since?

I don't wish for the hate between you to continue flowing unabated. In particular, it's a hatred that will not only cause one to find their own ruin, but will strongly attract direct reprisal from those murderers.
You're telling me all this on purpose, aren't you?
I won't deny that, Lillia. But...
Dr. Kal'tsit, you're leaving?
Ah, it's...
My student. No alarm, she came to pick me up.
Ah, I see, then... But, well, pick you up?
Oh, Emperor above... You're going after all then?
If you're going, please tell us! You cured my daughter, so at least let us send you off properly!
I will.
Ah, alright, alright, then we'll leave you two alone. You have to tell us before you go, please, Dr. Kal'tsit... don't forget.
The people here seem to like you.
They've shown enough goodwill already. I've merely done my work as a doctor.
They're the ones who live true Ursus lives. We've spent so long in the Research Institute, there's plenty we've forgotten.
Even positioning themselves near a cityway couldn't bring them opportunity, nor prosperity.
Precisely the opposite. A tyrant official with subordinate power took hold there, exploiting the poor folk to offer the Patrol Unit yet more convenient soil.
So they can't even find one run-of-the-mill doctor graduated from medical school. And one run-of-the-mill flu strain could kill a tender young girl.
Your horizons are vast. Yes, they've always been... You'd still treat a flu for someone? I thought...
You thought I was a scientist who gesticulated at papers and data.
To be honest, you're more a doctor than I even imagined.
Perhaps I always was a doctor. What changes is only the treatment subject and the locus to contend with.
It's why your students put so much faith in you.
Lillia.
How's your daughter?
I left her with a friend of mine. She has no connection to all this whatsoever.
You know... how young she is. She doesn't even speak yet. She hasn't begun to walk...
And I left behind my child just like that.
You could've chosen not to do this. If you wanted Louisa to live on, identity concealed—
You should know what I think, Director Kal'tsit.
I just want to try and stop you. But if not even your daughter can stall your step, perhaps my words won't reach you either.
That's right. I'm quite clear about that.
Winter's so cold this year.
Vampilov's father went mad from his son's death. He kept fowlbeasts penned in a loft by the lake, and every day would fantasize for a while he was going hunting with his son.
Ilia's child, Lyudmila, she's still young, but already comprehends certain, irreversible things. Nobody even knows how she's doing nowadays.
And Romanovich, too. His family sold off their whole estate, and left that city. Even if his brothers never believed in any accident, there were people to shut them up.
And for even more. The police announced it was an accident down to the finest thing.

In that 'list of accidental casualties,' not a single person had left Chernobog's research institute. An experimental accident leading to a chain reaction, and reported identities of bodies provided by the military.
But a kind of intuition tells me... some are alive.
Intuition?
Because afterwards, all relatives of the victims, more or less, received anonymous aid—as well as a warning.
I don't think that spineless, incompetent Marquess Boris, nor Sergei, that traitor, would have any mercy in them. So, someone else who knows what happened has to have survived.
But it took until today, three years later, before I found you, Director Kal'tsit. It took until today. You hid so well, you even fooled the Secret Police's eyes...
They rely too much on Ursus's latest technology for criminal investigation. It actually created an opening for me.
Of course... you're a hailed genius. An incomparable research leader. That's why everyone embraces you so.
—Please tell me, Lillia. How many people signed on to your plan?
Six or seven. Maybe even more; we don't dare meet together in public. We might be caught, used for leverage.
That includes me. All lost things in that 'accident' they can never have back.
When did you start plotting all of this?
From the moment I found out the Fourth Army's lackeys fired the first bolt in the Research Institute, mercilessly shredding the father of my children's throat. From that moment, I started.
Lillia...
Don't take me as a brave fool. Even if my health gives me so little time to work with you all... I've still accomplished one thing or another. Such as—
You found the whereabouts of Grand Duke Vanya. The Pine Valley Retreat, situated beyond any nomadic city.
Oh...

And this former Fourth Army staff officer was also highly likely to be a Grand Duke of Ursus actualizing the Sarcophagus incident, perhaps right in the frailest, weakest position of his endlessly long campaign career.
You want to conduct an assassination, Lillia, but this act of vengeance can still be considered 'bravely foolish.'
...Heh.
I can finally see why my husband... why that Astrov looked up to you so.
But you must know, Lillia, assassinating an old Grand Duke will have no significance at all.
Maybe from the start, Vanya, the man so decrepit he needs cardiac stimulant injections when he gives speeches, was not the real murderer.
I know, Kal'tsit, I know...
But do you really think when I entrusted Louisa to someone else, it was just for a rational ending, a so-called fair trial? As if you can't guess, Kal'tsit.
Blind revenge will only cause you to lack foresight.
Kal'tsit, Kal'tsit. Perhaps you couldn't understand.
Louisa never even learned to say 'papa.'
You can sit.
How long have you spent in here?
Not worth mentioning. Lillia. You look awful. You need to rest.
How did you find me?
Just sheer chance... if not for sheer chance, perhaps I'd have never learned you were still alive.
Lyudmila.
We didn't want our children coming to too much harm, so at first, we planned to look for Lyudmila.
But then, we learned from Lyudmila's housekeeper that someone was sustaining contact with them, aiding them. Someone anonymous, but she seemed to know it all, inside and out.
The same anonym had even set up a way for her to leave Ursus.
It was then I was sure that some were still alive. And to my knowledge, very few could accomplish something like that.
Have some tea.
Here I imagined you'd be itching to ask about my business at the Retreat.
Lillia, I imagine the one who's really itching here is you.
And you knew all along exactly where Vanya was... you're really just going to sit on the sidelines? You should know all the secrets he's holding onto!

The true secrets have already been sealed for eternity in that research institute, thanks to Ilia's perseverance. Even now, the Army haven't been able to find a single scrap of the gains they were drooling for.

And Grand Duke Vanya is incurably ill. He'll recuperate at Pine Valley every winter. Provided you have any way to sneak into a so-called high-class salon even once, it's not hard to come by the information.
He even brags all over about how the retreat is so beautiful and vast.
Correct. There is no shortage of Ursus soldiers with outstanding service there, and of course, bureaucrats and nobles too.
Infiltrating it and assassinating a Grand Duke would be as absurd as you, in Chernobog, charging into Marquess Boris's house with a knife, slitting his throat open and then escaping again untouched.
You're right. But for those 'bureaucrats and nobles,' security in there is still weak enough to make people restless.
I won't deny that. But how do you know that's not a signal, one that Grand Duke Vanya is no longer an important figure, or that...
Or that his fate has already been set by certain people? I've told you, Kal'tsit, what I want isn't a fair trial. To make it clear, what I long for is a lynching.
Now's not the time to discuss justice or morality, Kal'tsit. Only those who can rest easy at night enjoy using 'objectivity' as a shield.
Whether Grand Duke Vanya is censured by the military or the Emperor, becomes a scapegoat, stands the wrong side in political conflict, suffers banishment, is stowed away by the Army...
—No matter what, even if he dies miserably in the end at the gallows, what business is that of mine?
It was him who pushed for all of Chernobog to sink into deeper ruin, and all I know is he should pay the price. He should pay the price to us! He's the murderer who killed my child's father!
Kal'tsit!
I'm listening...
I've already figured out how to infiltrate the retreat... but I also need to admit, even if the security there is suspiciously weak, that doesn't mean any of us can pull this off smoothly.
I've found a breach. Someone who's been in charge of security at that noble's sanatorium for thirty-plus years, but hasn't had the guts to make much side-money off it for fear of the nobles.
I... prepared a huge sum, with my colleagues' help. That complete life failure, who we all saw could retire any day, caved in instantly.
We spent immense effort to gain his trust. I was simply the daughter of a country upstart, only wanting to intern at the retreat to get close with some noble or officer.
But...
You want me to help you.
Yes. Forging any identity has risks no matter what, but to my mind, the retreat and the information agency wouldn't be that tied up, so—
If even the Secret Police are fooled into thinking you're dead—
—then death will be your open road.

lines.st: In 1080, the Sarcophagus Incident's surviving scientist, Sergei, established a brand-new research institute.
The same year, Marquess Boris vastly expanded Chernobog's industrial region. The Fourth Army's Messengers, for the first time in history, found the Marquess's door was closed to them there.
Lyudmila was still young, and Louisa was but one amongst countless children, so Kal'tsit thought. She could comprehend that Lillia, as a mother, had complex, cruel expectations of these children.
And after the Chernobog Core City's engine underwent its umpteenth major maintenance, it still turned with a rumble.

eps.name: A Bolt From the Blue Oasis

lines.x: 17 Years Ago
1:37 P.M. \ Clear
Central Ursus, Pine Valley Retreat, Dining Hall
Sir, your coffee.
—Are you new?
I am, sir.
Then ask around about how I take my coffee. Bring it to me again once you let it cool!
You damned Feline—eugh, a Feline! You disgusting wretch! I didn't go to war to have a Feline take care of me!
Get out of my sight!
My utmost apologies, sir, this is my oversight.
Please don't tell the head caretaker. I'll prepare it again for you right away...
Hmph! If you don't want to lose your job, you have three minutes to do it over, properly.
Also... is something wrong with your eyes? If you don't know how to smile, I'll tear that face of yours off.
Please don't... I'm very sorry to displease you. I'll go and redo it right now.
Peh. Servants these days. Is a nice figure all they have to offer?
I am very sorry, sir. Please do not get angry.
It would be highly detrimental to your condition.
Out of the way!
Yes, yes. Very sorry.
Miss? Hey, Miss!
Are you referring to me?
Yeah, that's right. Miss, I saw the whole thing just now. Don't you lose heart.
That's a Patrol Unit head... you know, you could say, people like that have no right coming to the retreat.
He certainly isn't a noble... Does he not worry about offending others, acting so tyrannically?
They say some count's very fond of him... ah, and not just that. Coming here for his 'battlefield condition?' I heard that's hokum. Bet he hasn't stepped foot on a battlefield in his life.
I heard he helped the count do some dirty work, and got sent here so he could keep his head down. With a supporter like that, and with people like us, of course he lets all hell's bells loose.
...A count...
I never imagined a scoundrel like that could climb high enough to befriend a count.
That's how it is! Who knows when one day he'll be made some lord of somewhere! Dressed all clean and fine and sitting behind some enormous desk... agh.
Anyway, don't you get angry. Piss off people like that, and you'll wish it was an Originium Slug stuck to you instead. What on Terra is a fine young girl like you even doing, working out here...
Never tell anyone I told you this, and don't go asking about, or else you'll get burnt!
Yes... I understand. Thank you.
Ack. The next you see him, stay away a little. Heaven knows what he'll do to you—ah, is that the new doctor?
Kal'tsit.
Doctor.
Oh, oh, so you know each other... then I'll get out of your way. Definitely not break time, after all. Take care, and don't let the head caretaker see you.
She cares a lot about us newcomers. She's a good one.
You're right, Dr. Louisa. We cannot let her down.
Will you still be busy with work in a bit? There's some things I'd like to tell you.
Apologies, doctor... I still need to brew a new cup of coffee. If that count's pet doesn't continue to pick bones with me, I'll find you right after.
Alright, then... remember to be careful, Kal'tsit.
Dr. Louisa.
It's just us two right now, Kal'tsit. You should be using an alias.
'Louisa.' Borrowing your daughter's name. A disguise of that level is purely for humiliating the Secret Police.
In their eyes, I am already dead. And after this incident, I'll leave Ursus, and may not be able to return for a very long time.
Nobody will know the Grand Duke's death was a murder. The next time they come to the Grand Duke's side, they will only discover that in his old age, his breathing stopped.
Will Ursus technology not be enough to analyze the poison you've made?
At present, I assure you this exceeds their understanding of what a 'poison' is.
For how many years can your knowledge stay ahead of Ursus? Director?
The Empire's progress has already surpassed what I imagined, but these are not details we need to discuss right now.
Lillia, your hoped-for arbitration can only end in this way.
None of you have the means to seek further revenge; it is unreasonable. Only the paramount Emperor of Ursus would be capable of erasing the Grand Duke's honor, significance, and all else.
...I know...
Good. In that case, is this the place?
Yes. We have no idea what Grand Duke Vanya is chronically afflicted with.
But I found out from the head caretaker that every afternoon at three, Vanya will leave his own monitoring room, and come here to relax.
He sunbathes here until sunset. When he has the occasional guests, he'll have his dinner right here with them.
There's no way we can get close to the Grand Duke's room, but as for here...
We're standing right here.
Yes.
But he won't be without bodyguards. The whole corridor will be sealed off when the time comes.
Apart from any guests cleared to be with the Grand Duke, only his medical adviser, as well as the servants, are allowed to enter this area.
Even so, the Ursus nobles won't permit the workers to come to this balcony freely.
We've already waited for long enough. The longer we spend, the riskier this gets.
I understand. There has to be some gap, there has to be an opening... the Grand Duke's medical advisers work in shifts, so it might be me before we know it. Today, or perhaps tomorrow.
Lillia, tell me the truth.
After the Grand Duke dies, what will you do?
Why ask this now? We should be focusing on what's before us—
I'm hoping you can answer me with reality.
Before we reached Pine Valley, I received one final letter from Vampilov's family.
Sergei is still diligently serving Boris, and the city... has changed course. Perhaps this is the start of something changing.
They killed so many people... yet their conscience is still so clear. They rest so easily...
You found out about Sergei's betrayal.
But no excuse can bring back the dead.

Sergei can't offer any further scientific assistance. Perhaps in a few years, they'll formally call a halt to the project. And then? I just sit back and watch Sergei and Boris continue to collude, to trample over everything?
Though Grand Duke Vanya will be dead, you don't plan to stop.
There's no way I can pardon Sergei. The first time I met him, Astrov was drunk, hugging me so excited, telling me all about how he had such upstanding, excellent colleagues.
He looked forward to this project like no one else, and I supported him. I supported him with all I could.
Even up to now.
Director Kal'tsit, you know everything, don't you?
If you won't speak up, I'll return to Chernobog. I'll settle things once and for all.
Vampilov's family have an escape route to Sami ready for me, but maybe, I wouldn't get the chance to hitchhike with any Messengers. And you...
I do plan to.
Heh... of course.
I'm very well aware, I'm setting myself alight. So... Kal'tsit, no matter whether you plan to confess all the facts to me or not, at the very least there's one tiny thing I want you to agree to.
I know I'm doing something insane right now, and I know maybe you're the one in the right. But right now, I only have one request. Look after my daughter. Look after Louisa.

The next generation doesn't need to inherit the hassle of hatred... Even if nobody can escape this cycle in the end, at least promise me, Kal'tsit. Look after her. If you can, please look after Lyudmila too...
Dr. Louisa? Dr. Louisa, where are you?
Here! Please wait one moment!
Kal'tsit... I...
I promise you.
This is simply a mother's final wish. I will see Louisa grow up.
Thank you.
Astrov—the last time he held little Louisa's hand, he told me something, half-jokingly.
He said, he hoped little Louisa would become a medical researcher just like me, and when Louisa was old enough to go to school, he hoped you'd be her very first teacher.
I see...
Those were the very last words he said to his daughter before he suddenly went to the Institute that night.
Oh, doctor, here you are! Perfect, if you were any later, the head caretaker would've gone ballistic.
Hm? Doctor, what's got your eyes all red? Did something happen?
Nothing. What's the matter?
It's time to make rounds.
I wasn't even paying attention... right, I understand.
Is there some kind of commotion over there?
Yes, seems an old nobleman's come from far off. Don't know for what.
As long as the days go by smooth, that's what matters.
Hello—
Excuse me, we're currently making rounds... It seems you weren't on the list of scheduled visitors. Can I ask who you are?
Just an Ursus citizen here on a whim, looking to meet an old friend. Do carry on, madam.
I hope I don't bother any of you in your work.
Witte, are you off?
No, old friend, I'll see you again in a bit. You should be more mindful of your health, with winter here. Don't set off that asthma of yours.
As you were, madam. I'll be waiting in the hall.
Minister Witte!
Do watch out.
Ah, I, sorry...
The Grand Duke didn't know you were visiting. It's our negligence on the job to blame. As a token of apology, the Grand Duke would like you to have dinner with him.
We hope you understand, the Grand Duke is ill, and if not for that, he would've greeted you himself...
Huff.
Ah, I'm sorry, I'm greatly sorry...
What's wrong with you! In the presence of the Minister—
Oh, nothing, just some alcohol spilled on the bedsheets.
Calm down. Madam, what happened?
I'm sorry, the cap on the isopropyl bottle was too tight, and I applied too much force...
You could've just asked someone else to help.
Sorry, so sorry, I'll go and fetch a towel right now...
Poor her.
As for the Grand Duke's invitation, I do regret that my Messenger is still waiting for me, and I can't stay so long.

Do pass a message on to Vanya. Tell him to rest up with peace of mind here. Though he should remember, also, that this is a meritorious place for those Our Majesty gives favor, and not another social hub for the nobility to mingle in.
Ah... got it, got it... if you could just hold on... the Grand Duke will be very sad to hear.
This is official business. If I have to choose between Our Majesty and the Grand Duke, I'm forced to wrong the Grand Duke Vanya.
Ergh...! You should've spoke sooner! If that's so, the Grand Duke will surely empathize with you.
May your road be evergreen.
Send him my regards.
Dr. Louisa, what's wrong...?
Kal'tsit, who is he?
Viscount Islam Witte.
You recognize him...?
Ursus's new Minister of Finance, a fresh noble with deep favor from the Emperor.
He...
It's alright, Dr. Louisa.
I have only surface knowledge of his character, and don't know specifically how he made his fortune. But what I do know is, he's a present pillar who the Emperor of Ursus has personally assisted.
And young elites like him can only have their sights set on the whole of Ursus. To the waves of crisis crashing against the Empire, the crises of Chernobog are mere tiny ripples.
Even a Grand Duke of Ursus—
—Hold on... hold on.
The Minister of Finance came to Pine Valley, yet has no armed entourage?
Oh... I don't think so.
13 Years Ago
8:09 P.M. \ Heavy snow
Victoria Border Autonomous Region, County of Toron, Vincent Manor
What a splendid blizzard eve! Ladies and gentlemen, welcome, welcome, please come in. Allow the servants to brush the snow off your tops, and thaw yourselves by the brazier.
A good evening to you, Count. Truly a magnificent soirée, this is.
Welcome, welcome, Lady Diana. To meet you on the eve of the New Year is my honor entirely.
Nonsense. Now take a look, Count. I've a present for you, all the way from Kazimierz.
What a beautiful pendant! My greatest thanks, Lady Diana, and send my well-wishes to your husband. Now, hurry along inside, we can't have the snow burying your gorgeous looks.
Hah! You speak as fine a line as ever.
Oh-ho, look who's here! If it isn't our 'Baron of Rye!'
I could never shoulder a title as hefty as Baron, but here I am anyway, the pure nerve!
See what you're saying! We all respect you from the bottom of our hearts. Who spurns the man who feeds his people well, now? Ha ha ha!

Ah, you're quite right. Once harvest comes next year, I'd be honored if you visit my humble home. The tallest windmills in Toron, the scent of flour everywhere. A stay sure to enchant you, if I do say so myself.
Quite good! This blizzard shall pass, and next year will come trotting in right behind it with fine, fine harvests.
My gratitude for your kind words.
Head in and warm yourself up, now. We're serving the spiced bread you so love tonight—of course, baked with most exquisite, fragrant flour, all sourced from your farms.
How about you? Won't you come in? It's almost time... Let your good servants welcome the remaining guests.
Perhaps you speak sense, but no matter. I like to greet my invitees personally. They're all the most adorable people, whether noble or merchant, cleric or soldier.
Toron is a fine, fine place, isn't it?
Why, you character, you...
Now, let me see, we still have the Mister and Misses Arizona, that admirable lighthouse operator, and... hm?
E-hem.

I'd heard from my most venerable fellow student, that gifted orator Thomson, that he had recently taken ill, and I couldn't brook the thought of him facing a blizzard for a banquet. Hence my specifically sending for a Messenger, to send him my well-wishes—
And yet, imagine my surprise to see—what seems to be a sneaky darling of a Messenger right here!
Here-here!
Eek!
Ah-ha, Mistress Heidi, how long since we last met?
Uncle Vincent, don't ruffle up my hair! I had to spend so long making it!
Fine, fine. Ah, little Heidi, you've grown so much. What a dashing lady you are now.
However! Your father never sent word you'd be arriving today. Be candid with your uncle now, have you come on the sly?
And I say, you're still quite young. If your father saw your dress tonight, you'd be in for the nag, wouldn't you?
Uncle! I'd hate him nagging at me. That's why I slipped off in the first place!

I heard you called out a gallery of VIPs and bright minds tonight, and I couldn't help my curiosity. Not to mention, my papa always wants me to broaden my scope, get out beyond the classroom, and isn't this the finest opportunity!

And you swear you're not here to see the boys, hoping for a chance meeting? You know, your father's told me, you've been absorbed in all those tawdry romance novels lately, and it's been worrying him so.
How—How—How could I? I'm not such a naive girl now, ah ha ha, ha ha.

Your father worded it very well. Romanticism is all and well, but writing aiming to be consumed, pulling wool over the truths of life, so as to curry favor with readers and wring the last drop of worth from its material; there's nothing of value in it.
In that regard, all of Toron ought to be learning from your father.
Ahem, Uncle, let's drop the subject now. I feel embarrassed hearing this...

And honestly, my papa. Telling people off left and right when he was young, and in the end he made himself a businessman, made himself a huge fortune... The old guard in the association constantly joke about him. It drives me mad.

Some things up Life's sleeve you just can't refuse. Oh, well, imagine Thomson did it for you two ladies. Back when he was younger, he'd find any chance to grumble about his unrecognized talent to me. How's his health been lately?
Just his chronic pains. Once winter's gone, he'll be live and well as ever.
So you availed yourself while your parents couldn't watch you, made off with your mother's clothes, and came to join the festivities?
Right—ah, no, I came on educational purposes, just as my father guided me.
Heidi...
Heh heh. How about you just never saw me?
You've been quite too headstrong this time. Look at this awful snow today. Just suppose something had happened to you on the way, how would I ever explain to your father?
Ha, ha ha... well! Uncle, you see, it's so late now anyway. I can't just find a car and head back now, can I?
Oh, dear, child. Quick, in. Don't you freeze now.
Hooray! You're the best, Uncle!
Er... but who are you still waiting for? Is there still some guest noble enough to wait like a snowman at the door?
Quite. An outstanding Laterano cleric.
Last year, your aunt caught some illness unawares, had a fever that wouldn't abate, and it's thanks to that cleric that it vanished.
We struck up concord afterwards, and I paid frequent visits. This cleric's not just fine with medicine, but has original views on all walks of life. Truly a mind-opener.
Huh... Well, if you vouch for them like this, they must be really incredible, right?
But of course. If a person of those talents could swear loyalty to the Vincent family, it'd bring a deal of vigor to building the local land.
I even had every intent to invite that cleric along as a friend on my journey to Londinium, and your aunt would've appreciated the diversion, but would you know our rotten luck? Out on business.
So today, mark my words, I'll be introducing this insightful scholar to one and all.
Uncle... is that Laterano cleric—a gentleman by any chance? A refined, mannerly Sankta, maybe? On the older side, or?
There it is, you little scamp! Swearing your pure intentions, even!
Just asking!
Don't get too excited. She's a meritorious woman. And she isn't a Sankta. Very rare to see, but all suggests she's a Feline cleric. I truly do appreciate people like her.
Ahem...!
U—Uncle, I didn't hear any of that, and I won't breathe a word to Auntie, you can cross my heart.
And what silly ideas have you gotten! I really ought to make Thomson give you a proper word or three!
My apologies, Sir Vincent.
Might I have arrived late?
Ah, Cleric Kal'tsit, you're finally here. Er, your mode of dress...

I was deeply honored by your invitation, but with sudden matters to attend to, the monastery could not prepare suitable evening attire for me. They found only this outfit buried in the storeroom. I hope you shan't mind.
Oh, quite naturally, I shan't. I'd go so far as to say your attire is utterly... utterly, formidably gallant.
Heidi, you don't just stand there. Here, this is Cleric Kal'tsit. Give her a greeting, and then hurry inside quickly.
Heidi?
Miss Heidi, it's good to meet you.
Ah—er—uh, th—um, I'm Heidi—
I know it already. An adorable name.
—So you're—Cleric Kal'tsit, is that right? I often hear my uncle mention you.
The pleasure is mine.
Cleric Kal'tsit, I imagine you know of Mr. Thomson too? Quite, this is Heidi Thomson before you, that strange fellow's daughter.
I see. Your father is a person worthy of respect.
Right, then, do come in, Cleric.
Thank you again for your generosity, Sir Vincent.
Oh, did you know the Count went to Londinium? Have you ever been in that city before?
Don't remind me! I once had everything packed up ready, and then! A Catastrophe Messenger came trotting along to say: the road ahead's blocked, you all go back.
I was beside myself returning home. Immediately had a great bout of illness.
Oh, my, how dearly wretched.
Is that auspicious poet coming today? I've a few works I wrote in private, and I'd hoped he could do the honor of appraising them.
Perish the thought! Have you forgotten just how many manuscripts from 'thinkers' Thomson's torn up in front of the Count?
Oh, I remember. Thomson's ruthless. He even said himself, 'The clash of ideas ought to be a ruthless thing.' Truly frightening, he is.
It's not a be-all nor end-all to show off.
My, my, but the charm's all in young men like that, isn't it? Mettle to test, romantic and free.
Of course I admit, I once admired Thomson for those qualities. But in front of the Count, Thomson still can't shake off his own deliberate arrogance.
Deliberate? Thomson?
Isn't it obvious? He regards our esteemed Count, after all. Forget just nobility and status; in these last years, the Count's governed his land with perfect order. It's plain for all of us to see.
Hoh! Toron's not his land in name, mind, it's for all of us to manage, but I'll admit the Count deserves his reputation for leading! I do have to respect the man!
And lest we forget, he's had multiple invitations to the gatherings at Londinium. Don't you think our little place—allow me to admit our border situation—deserves some sense of pride?
Hah, nobles.
My, let's not make the topic so stiff. We're all people of Victoria, aren't we?
Ha! People! Ever since that outrageous hanging, we can barely claim to know whose people we are!
Simmer down. We all regret Our Majesty's departure, but we can still drink a few toasts here today, you see?
In other words, today's a happy day. Don't bring up anything so frightful—look, here comes the Count.
I overheard your conversation. Very intriguing, Richard. I'd much like to take a look at your writings.
Oh, poppycock. Good Count, in comparison to you, my little effort pales like a man under a skyscraper.
That's right, Vincent, hurry and tell us of your trip to Londinium, let us all hear it. Who did you meet? Any Grand Dukes of living legend?
In due time, everyone, in due time. The night has just begun. Don't forget, today is a day of festival celebration.
Allow me to introduce to you all two special guests of ours.
Cleric Kal'tsit, do come.
Good evening, honorable guests. Allow me to give my most sincere respects to you all.
What, now! By your appearance... my, you're a lady? Oh, what a mighty shame...
I think it's not much of a shame at all. Cleric Kal'tsit, good evening.
Oh—oh—Cleric Kal'tsit, I believe I remember the Count speaking of you. I hear you treated his wife, and so garnered his good favor?
With regards to the Count's wife, that was no light affair.
Do tell us about where you're from. Laterano clerics who aren't Sankta are so rare. My, let alone ones so... charming as you.
Beautiful miss, where do you come from? And where will you go?
Aren't I the one asking? Don't you interrupt.
The Count gives word that you're a pillar of talent. Do you have any inclination for philosophy or the arts? Any unique insights, perhaps?
You flatter me. It was only chance I aided the Count's wife. I have the distinction of his recognition, that's all.
Oh—if you're familiar with the Count, surely you know Sir Thomson too, then?
Of course. Sir Thomson is a rarity of a scholar in Toron. I often hear the Count talk of him.
Then things couldn't align better. Servant! Please fetch the wine I brought. I wish to have a proper causerie with the Count and the Cleric!
Don't you dominate our soirée, you egomaniac. Can't we all just chat about something fun? For example, that fashion walk at Londinium...
Fine by me. Everyone, just let me rest my legs for a while here. I'll be soaking in the knowledge.
Now, aren't you a lazy one. Your estate would be doubled by now if not for that!
Three wineries still isn't enough?
Oh, right, you mentioned you had another guest. I don't know if—
Good evening, Misters and Misses.
Heidi! What are you doing here!
Little Heidi, you're very prettily dressed today... that bastard Thomson. I fear for all my life, it's children alone where I'll never compare to him.
Now, now. Heidi isn't even of drinking age. None of you hand her a wine glass. Servants, if you could pour her a cream soda.
Don't always treat me like a child, Uncle Vincent.
But drinking laws are drinking laws. Your father specifically warned me. After all, you're at that age...
(Pout...)
Alright, alright. Strike the cream soda and replace it with ginger ale.
I don't need you to coddle me like this!
Ha ha, little Heidi's eager to grow up.
It's sad. No father wants to see his daughter grow up this quickly. I was like that at her age too. Only wanted to buy some jewelry, and my father gave me a tongue-lashing for it.
Even if you won't let me drink, at least, can I join the dinner party? Let's see... I'll sit right here!
It would be my honor, Miss Heidi. Allow me to hold your hat for you.
Ah... no—no need, I'll be fine with it on...
Good, then. Heidi, don't forget that you came here tonight in secret. In a while, you be good and sleep for the night upstairs.
Mnngh...
You dress very much like your mother, girl.
But right now—
Mnnngh...!!
Cleric Kal'tsit, do you think I'm still a child too?
To you, I see no reason why it should matter.
Let me pour your glass for you. Here's your ginger ale.
Th... thank you...
Is it to your liking?
It's actually quite a nice drink.
Oh, my, there is no alcohol in ginger ale, is there? What's gotten your face red?
The girl's wary of strangers. It's nothing new.
Come, friends! Seeing as we're all present, let us officially begin!
You're most correct! The height of Leithanien's arts capture all hearts alike, but those Leithanians are far too pompous about themselves! What affords them to look down on us!

Leithanien, oh, Leithanien. They've had too much misfortune... I haven't stopped hearing Witch King stories since I was a baby. Who knows if the Twin Empresses will have managed to sort out that king by the time I visit the place!
Who would visit knowing the terror of the Witch King...
Unlike the monarchs who ascended young, the Witch King became emperor at nearly fifty years of age, and then governed Leithanien for many more.
Undoubtedly, the Witch King is the greatest Caster in all our lands. He turned himself into a freak of nature!
He's just like those horrible stories depicted him. His horns all bent into strange shapes, emitting this strange energy...

Oh! I've never seen the Witch King shown in a newspaper before—*belch*—Sorry there, this wine's just too fine. But I have seen photos of the Twin Empresses, come to think of it. One gleaming gold, one dark as night.

Leithanien is in flux. Presumably, the Witch King rarely leaves his tower. From start to finish, he's gazed dispassionately at Leithanien, and even when he grants an audience, it's as he casts a grand shadow from atop his tower.
Yet the last time he touched the ground, he was morphed beyond recognition. His wretchedness, his two horns cut off by the Twin Empresses apiece, drew true sighs of lament.
The full picture of that coup d'etat is still unclear to us, but to my knowledge, even our most heroically adored Wanderer would never match the Witch King's true strength.
This being why when the Empresses' Voice arrived at Victoria escorted by the knights, the Grand Dukes were all still willing to suspend their quarrels, and treat each other with respect.
Pardon? It sounds as though—you've been to Londinium before?
I arrived from there. It was a local Sankta's enlightening me that led me on this path.
Ha-ha, Kal'tsit, didn't I say? These are all wonderful friends of mine. See just how popular you are.
Truth is, I have a girl about the same age as you. She only knows how to splash my hard-earned money away all the damned day... How nice it'd be if she could learn from you.
Getting to know you tonight has been a truly wonderful time. Can I ask if you're still single, dear Kal'tsit?
Cleric Kal'tsit!
Oh, Heidi, don't cut in so randomly. You should be heading upstairs to turn in about now.
Allow her to speak, Count. In this day and age, children are always much more mature than we imagine.
Hm.
Cleric Kal'tsit... have—can I ask if you've been to Ursus before?
Oh, that nation rife with rebellion and violence.
When I was studying abroad, I spent some time at Deity Grypherburg. Are you interested in Ursus?
Yes!
Papa once gave me a few books to read, by an Ursus author...
Ursus only ever produces two kinds of arts: raucous sensationalism, or true eternal greats.
If Thomson liked it, chances are it was the latter, wasn't it?
Hmph. You lot really do trust him.

Papa really put him in high esteem. He even wanted to go to Deity Grypherburg himself to visit him, but business always meant he had to put it off, and this year with his old injuries flaring up again, he wasn't able to go.
Your father was injured?
That's right. He broke his knee, and then put off surgery for too long, so it's not fully treated.

I warned him. Ah, no idea what happened last year. He came scrabbling back with wounds all over, and all he'd say was he ran into a robber and dropped off a cliff. Wasn't interested in saying much else.
Do you want to go to Ursus? Miss Heidi?
Now that won't do! The death of the last Emperor and the rebellions had a profound effect on the country. Should you want to study there, you'd do well to wait a few years first.
I'm just talking about it... What impression do you have of the country?
I'll tell you a story.
It's the story of how Ursus's Sixth Army were annihilated, without a trace, by a bottle of vodka.
Well, this rumor doesn't sound plausible at all.
When any event is boiled down to its cause, its conclusion tends to sound unexpected. We're all used to the process, aren't we?
That does make sense.
It began with a ruffian from the Army 'collecting rent' in one of their garrison regions, and without realizing, he menaced a noble.

That act fermented the whole way through. The Sixth Army then were always considered utterly corrupt, the old nobles' power rooted too deep, while the Fifth Army were unconditionally obedient to the young Emperor and the brand new State Council.

We have no way to know for sure who fired the first shot, but as for the outcome, the Fifth Army ultimately seized the chance to revolt, and in between two cities' paths, started a direct firefight with the Sixth Army.
The prologue to 'The Great Rebellion' comes in many editions. I've definitely heard a few things of the Sixth Army's varied deeds.
They fought just like that?
If it were that simple, then it wouldn't have been dubbed 'The Great Rebellion.'

Being a border guard outfit, the Fourth Army, with a domain of authority and influence decidedly not in the hinterlands, suddenly switched sides to support the State Council, and proactively declared no relationship to the 'traitors' in the Sixth Army.
After the execution of a few prepared scapegoats, the old nobility had only the Sixth and Eighth Armies left in hand.
Come winter the following year, the nobility backing the Eighth Army pulled that same trick, capitulating alike to the Emperor.
And so, the bitter fate of the Sixth Army who had staked it all on one throw, we can well imagine.
And the cause of all of this—very simple, one we all know. Simply a drunk ruffian, taking the vodka bottle he had in hand to attack a young noble.
A marvelous story, Cleric Kal'tsit, and might I even confirm, this tale is little removed from the reality at all.
Heidi, I would not tell you that 'this is how Ursus is.' Every civilization in this world deserves you seeing it for yourself. They should be appreciated, and have to be reflected upon.
In that Ursus you admire, what have you seen?

Perhaps the farms and public houses, perhaps the grand heroes, perhaps the highs and lows of life in Deity Grypherburg's opera house. But what I've seen is the Great Rebellion's aftermath, an age on the verge of collapse.
Might Victoria have such an age of its own someday?
I will not guarantee it either way, Miss Heidi.
How could it! Here, you little child. Are you afraid now, because of Cleric Kal'tsit's smidgen story of Ursus?
I've no intent to insult the Ursus people, but the Empire of Ursus is unarguably a savage, unreasonable nation.
But right now, isn't Londinium just as—
—Don't even say it. Though it pains us all that it happened, did Victoria waver for even a moment?
Alright, alright, Count, seeing as the topic's come around, I think it's high time you told us about that trip to Londinium, yes? That's what we're most interested in.
What high nobility did you meet?
I'd be honored to tell. Truth is, on the way, the Duke of Normandy personally invited me to a shindig, together at his flagship...
What! And you managed to keep from telling us all the way to today...!
My, it was the Duke of Normandy himself... He always did detest Leithanians. Did he and the envoys of the Empresses' Voice have a scuffle?
No, but by his retainer's words, the good Duke never once made eye contact with those gorgeous envoys that evening.
Ah-ha, now that's the proper manner of a 'Duke.'
Ursus's theatrical Grand Dukes might number more in hundreds than tens—alright, maybe not that many, but they are quite unchecked, and if I might add, somewhat incompetent too.
That's a form of arrogance, but I must say, everyone who's experienced the Londinium manner for themselves would echo the sentiment.

I trust the Duke of Normandy need only rely on his own power to make those Leithanian greed-blinded extremists know their place for a few dozen years. Let alone the fact we've certainly more than a single Duke...
Tuckered out, Heidi?
Hm? No... I'm still thinking about what Cleric Kal'tsit said. Could I talk with her alone for a bit?
Oh, if we're to talk Londinium without Kal'tsit, it would certainly be much duller.
You flatter me.
Now, no rush. I see the Baron of Rye is calling at me, so I'll need to chit-chat with him now. I assure you, I'll be back shortly to tell the full tale.
Does this all bore you? Miss Heidi?
Huh? Ah... no, it doesn't, I'm alright.
